top_stories.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
top_stories.summary: The article addresses a critical security gap where OAuth tokens granted to third-party apps connected to Google or Microsoft persist indefinitely without expiration or monitoring. These tokens bypass MFA and perimeter controls, creating an unmanaged attack surface that grows with every new AI tool or workflow automation employees authorize.

top_stories.title: Omada Launches Omada Identity Cloud Private for Regulated Enterprises and Government Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched "Omada Identity Cloud Private," a new deployment option that runs the full Omada Identity Cloud platform within the customer's own tenant. The offering is targeted at regulated enterprises and government organizations requiring strict data sovereignty and auditability boundaries.

top_stories.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
top_stories.summary: Attackers are abusing Windows Phone Link to intercept SMS messages and bypass two-factor authentication using a remote access trojan called CloudZ RAT and a new plugin named Pheno. The attack hijacks the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones, allowing threat actors to steal authentication codes in a hard-to-detect manner.

top_stories.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits rapidly appearing in the wild. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation activity has been ongoing for at least a month prior to disclosure, putting millions of cPanel-managed systems at risk.

top_stories.title: CISA gives feds four days to patch Ivanti flaw exploited as zero-day
top_stories.summary: CISA has mandated U.S. federal agencies patch a high-severity zero-day vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) within four days. The flaw is actively being exploited in zero-day attacks, triggering the urgent directive under CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

news.title: Helping North Korean IT remote workers is becoming a fast track to prison
news.summary: Two U.S. nationals were sentenced to 18 months in prison for operating "laptop farms" that helped North Korean IT workers fraudulently gain employment at nearly 70 American companies, generating over $1.2 million for Pyongyang. The cases highlight identity fraud and workforce authentication vulnerabilities, as the schemes relied on concealing the true identities of remote workers to bypass employer verification processes.

news.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
news.summary: Researchers have disclosed a new Linux backdoor called PamDOORa, sold on a Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600, which abuses Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) to steal SSH credentials. The toolkit enables persistent SSH access via a hardcoded magic password and specific TCP port combination, representing a direct attack against Linux authentication infrastructure.

news.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
news.summary: Researchers have disclosed PCPJack, a credential theft framework that targets cloud infrastructure by harvesting credentials from cloud, container, developer, productivity, and financial services. The malware exploits five CVEs to spread worm-like across cloud systems and exfiltrates stolen credentials through attacker-controlled infrastructure.

news.title: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
news.summary: Microsoft published a blog post for World Passkey Day highlighting its efforts to advance passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords. The post covers Microsoft's initiatives around passwordless authentication, phishing risk reduction, and simplified sign-in experiences.

news.title: 5 leading enterprise password managers to consider
news.summary: The article reviews five enterprise password managers, outlining key features and capabilities that administrators should look for. It covers available options in the market for organizations seeking enterprise-grade credential management solutions.

news.title: Semperis Names John Podboy Chief Information Security Officer
news.summary: Semperis has appointed John Podboy as its Chief Information Security Officer. Podboy will focus on risk, resilience, compliance, and agentic AI-driven cyber defense for the identity-driven cyber resilience company.

news.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
news.summary: Gartner has published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, confirming that enterprise AI agent adoption is outpacing governance and policy controls. The report addresses the identity security challenge of governing AI agents operating inside enterprise perimeters, a growing concern for IAM professionals managing non-human identities and access controls.

news.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
news.summary: Researchers have disclosed details of an attack using CloudZ RAT and a previously undocumented plugin called Pheno to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs). The attack exploited Windows Phone Link to facilitate credential theft, directly targeting authentication factors including OTPs.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server is now generally available
news.summary: AWS has announced the general availability of the AWS MCP Server, a managed remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to give AI agents and coding assistants authenticated access to AWS services. The server provides secure, authenticated connectivity as part of AWS's Agent Toolkit offering.

news.title: Keeper Security Research Reveals 89% of IT Leaders Struggle to Manage Growing Identity Footprint Amid AI Expansion
news.summary: Keeper Security released a global study of 3,200 cybersecurity decision-makers finding that 89% of IT leaders struggle to manage growing identity footprints amid AI expansion. The research reports that 96% of respondents say disconnected identity tools are creating exploitable security gaps, with AI adoption identified as a key driver of identity sprawl.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing an attacker with administrative privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability highlights risks for enterprise environments where credential theft could enable lateral movement or further attacks.

news.title: Microsoft Details Phishing Campaign Targeting 35,000 Users Across 26 Countries
news.summary: Microsoft disclosed details of a large-scale credential theft campaign observed between April 14-16, 2026, that targeted over 35,000 users across more than 13,000 organizations in 26 countries. The multi-stage attack used code of conduct-themed lures and legitimate email services to redirect users to attacker-controlled domains and steal authentication tokens.

news.title: Modernize your workflows: Amazon WorkSpaces now gives AI agents their own desktop (preview)
news.summary: Amazon WorkSpaces has launched a preview feature allowing AI agents to operate legacy desktop applications using IAM authentication, MCP support, and computer vision. The feature uses IAM authentication as part of its security framework to enable AI agents to access desktop environments without requiring API integration or application modernization.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The group executed SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and stole tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
